introSection: Breezer, originally known as a rendition of L.A Thompson Scenic Railway, Russian Railway, was a wooden roller coaster built in 1909 at Ontario Beach Park. The roller coaster was designed by L.A. Thompson and constructed by Arthur F. Turpin, a former business relation of Thompson's. Breezer operated at the park until its closure in 1919, the same day as the closure of the Ontario Beach Park amusement park. Ontario Beach Park, a city owned and operated park, continues to operate as a general public park today and features the original and Historic Dentzel Carousel which is still in operation today.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Breezer featured several dips and turns with a finale through a banked dark tunnel before returning riders to the station. The tunnel featured a series of loud lion roars for an added thrill.

fullText:
Breezer, originally known as a rendition of L.A Thompson Scenic Railway, Russian Railway, was a wooden roller coaster built in 1909 at Ontario Beach Park. The roller coaster was designed by L.A. Thompson and constructed by Arthur F. Turpin, a former business relation of Thompson's. Breezer operated at the park until its closure in 1919, the same day as the closure of the Ontario Beach Park amusement park. Ontario Beach Park, a city owned and operated park, continues to operate as a general public park today and features the original and Historic Dentzel Carousel which is still in operation today.

Breezer featured several dips and turns with a finale through a banked dark tunnel before returning riders to the station. The tunnel featured a series of loud lion roars for an added thrill.
